content_signals.body_excerpt: Switch proofs history defaults to RocksDB, add the RocksDB-backed storage implementation, and add write benchmark coverage.

## Summary

This refactor moves duplicated trace capture setup into the protocol test utilities and reuses it from derive tests. It generates the repeated rollup fork activation methods from a shared macro while preserving the public method names. It also adds a shared CLI entrypoint macro for binaries that parse Clap args, run the command, and exit on errors.

## Summary

This removes the rollup config registry from base-common-chains and derives rollup configs directly from ChainConfig instead. It adds a rollup_config! macro that accepts chain IDs, alloy Chain values, or ChainConfig values while preserving unknown-chain fallback behavior. The downstream call sites now use either the macro for dynamic inputs or hardcoded ChainConfig access for fixed Base networks.

## Summary

This refactor adds a crate-local helper macro for Alloy contract calls in base-proof-contracts. The contract clients now share the same error mapping path for failed calls while preserving the existing context strings and validation behavior. It keeps the change separate from the broader common macro cleanup PR.

## Summary

This refactors the Base Azul action tests to share their common harness setup, transaction creation, and derivation assertions. The probe-specific CLZ, P256, and MODEXP tests now focus on their individual bytecode, calls, and storage checks while reusing a single test environment helper.

## Summary

This adds a `base update` subcommand that delegates verified binary updates to `baseup` in the same install directory as the running executable. It also updates `baseup` to install the unified `base` binary, verify signed release archives, and optionally check GitHub SLSA provenance. The release workflow now publishes the `base` artifact with checksums, GPG signatures, and attestations so the command has a signed artifact to install.
commit_subjects: fix(cli): satisfy update docs clippy lint

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This refactors batcher subscription wrappers to share generic stream, keepalive, and pending subscription implementations. The L2 block and L1 head subscription APIs remain unchanged while the duplicated WebSocket and no-op wrapper mechanics move into base-batcher-source.

key_prs.author: niran

content_signals.body_excerpt:
> **Draft — alternative to #2366. Only one of these will land.** This PR ships an XEN-specific payload with bundled `State`/`Worker`/`Multicall` contracts. #2366 instead reuses the already-deployed `Simulator` from `base/benchmark` and exposes its full config in YAML.

## Summary

- Adds a `xen` YAML transaction type that mirrors XEN's `bulkClaimRank` shape: each tx loops N CREATE2 `Worker` deployments, and each worker writes a unique slot in an underlying `State` contract — N new account-trie entries plus N unique storage writes per tx, with no merging in `HashedPostState`.
- Bundles minimal `State.sol`, `Worker.sol`, `Multicall.sol` sources (with a self-contained Foundry project) and embeds their compiled bytecode as `&'static [u8]` constants on `XenContracts`. Follows the `LooperPayload` pattern: deploy manually, pass the addresses via YAML.
- Unblocks the `xen` performance-baseline scenario.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo +nightly fmt --all`
- [x] `cargo +nightly clippy -p base-load-tests -p basectl --all-features` (workspace-wide clippy blocked locally by an unrelated risc0 metal-kernel toolchain issue)
- [x] `cargo nextest run -p base-load-tests` — 32/32 pass, including new Y...
commit_subjects: feat(load-tests): add XEN-shape payload for storage-trie + account-creation stress

key_prs.title: feat(load-tests): add XEN-shape payload for storage-trie + account-creation stress

key_prs.author: niran

content_signals.body_excerpt:
> **Draft — alternative to #2363. Only one of these will land.** This PR reuses the already-deployed `Simulator` contract from `base/benchmark` and exposes its full config in YAML, so XEN-shape becomes `create_accounts: N, create_storage: N` with no new contracts embedded in this crate. #2363 instead ships XEN-specific `State`/`Worker`/`Multicall` Solidity.

## Summary

- Adds a `simulator` YAML transaction type that calls `Simulator.run(SimulatorConfig)` on the deployed `Simulator` contract from `base/benchmark` (already deployed on Sepolia Alpha, Base Sepolia, and Mainnet).
- Each YAML field maps 1:1 to the on-chain `SimulatorConfig` struct, so workloads — account-trie growth, storage-trie growth, updates, deletes, precompile mixes — can be described entirely in YAML with no new contracts embedded in this crate.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo +nightly fmt --all`
- [x] `cargo +nightly clippy -p base-load-tests -p basectl --all-features`
- [x] `cargo nextest run -p base-load-tests` — 31/31 pass, including new YAML round-trip + payload tests
- [ ] End-to-end run against sepolia / sepolia alpha pointing at the deployed Simulator addresses

type=routine
risk=low
impact=sev5
commit_subjects: feat(load-tests): add Simulator-based payload for general resource stress

key_prs.title: feat(load-tests): add Simulator-based payload for general resource stress

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

- New top-level `fresh_recipients: bool` config option. When `true`, every transaction draws a freshly derived recipient signing key from a `KeyStream` (mnemonic or seed, advanced past the sender pool) instead of cycling through the sender accounts.
- Unblocks the `dust` performance-baseline scenario, which needs maximum account-trie fan-out per gas (one new account-trie entry per send).
- Recovery is deterministic: the runner prints `recipient_offset` at startup, and any recipient can be regenerated with `AccountPool::from_mnemonic(mnemonic, n, recipient_offset)` or `AccountPool::with_offset(seed, n, recipient_offset)`.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo +nightly fmt --all`
- [x] `cargo +nightly clippy -p base-load-tests --lib --tests --all-features` (workspace clippy not run locally — `risc0-sys` build needs the macOS `metal` developer tool, unrelated to this change)
- [x] `cargo nextest run -p base-load-tests` — 35/35 pass, including new `key_stream_seed_matches_account_pool` and `key_stream_mnemonic_matches_account_pool` recovery-contract tests
- [ ] Smoke against devnet with `fresh_recipients: true` and a `transfer` payload

type=routine
risk=low
impact=sev5
commit_subjects: feat(load-tests): add fresh-recipient mode for account-trie fan-out

closed_unmerged.title: feat(load-tests): add fresh-recipient mode for account-trie fan-out

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

Adds BCP-0003 (Virtual Address Deposit Routing) to the specs site alongside existing BCPs. The proposal introduces a virtual address scheme that routes token deposits directly to a registered master address at the protocol layer, eliminating the need for sweep transactions. It carries a hard prerequisite on an enshrined Base token standard implemented as a precompile, analogous to Tempo's TIP-1022. The BCP is listed with Draft status on the BCP index page.
commit_subjects: docs(bcps): add BCP-0003 virtual address deposit routing

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

Adds action tests demonstrating that the op-batcher fails to manage batches correctly when the base-consensus sequencer reports a stale `safe_l2` in `optimism_syncStatus`. The root cause is that `EngineSyncState::safe_head()` stays at genesis for sequencer-built blocks because `InsertTask` only sets `safe_head` when `is_payload_safe` is true (derived blocks only). Four tests cover the stale catchup-from-genesis failure, the correct catchup with a live safe head feed, the encoder pruning gap, and a focused unit test proving the `EngineSyncState` root cause. These tests document the behavior that PR #2362 fixes by adding the `local_safe_head` field.
commit_subjects: test(batcher): add local safe head devnet tests
test(devnet): add local safe head stall test against real consensus RPC
test(devnet): run Go op-batcher against base-consensus, show stale safe_l2
fix(devnet): assert missing local_safe_l2 field, not stale safe_l2

draft.title: test(batcher): Local Safe Head Devnet Tests

content_signals.body_excerpt:
### Description
With https://github.com/base/base/pull/2742 and https://github.com/base/base/pull/2743 reducing the number of blocks that can be inserted concurrently. We should prefetch blocks from the source node to ensure we don't end up paying 5-10ms per block insert.

This PR adds prefetching of block data to follow mode.

### Description
The engine api is optimization for serial insertion (with sparse trie cache etc). This PR waits for the previous insert to complete before continuing.
commit_subjects: chore(follow): wait for inserts to complete

draft.title: chore(follow): wait for inserts to complete

content_signals.body_excerpt:
### Description
Reduce max ahead proofs default. With the migration to rocks db proof insertion is now quick, there's no real reason to allow the node to get 512 blocks (~20m ahead).

Allowing the node to be 512 blocks ahead also has issues if there's a failure, for example it takes ~1m to load/calc/insert a ~20m old block.
commit_subjects: chore(follow): reduce max ahead proofs default

draft.title: chore(follow): reduce max ahead proofs default
